CBAM Resources

Frequently asked questions, terminology glossary, and official EU documentation links.

FAQGlossaryOfficial Documents

Frequently Asked Questions

The Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) is the EU's carbon tax on imports. It requires importers of certain carbon-intensive goods (cement, iron/steel, aluminum, fertilizers, electricity, hydrogen) to purchase certificates matching the embedded carbon emissions in those goods. The price of certificates mirrors the EU ETS carbon price.

CBAM Glossary

TermDefinition
CBAMCarbon Border Adjustment Mechanism - EU import carbon tariff
EU ETSEuropean Union Emissions Trading System - carbon market for EU industries
tCO₂eTonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent - standard emission unit
HS CodeHarmonized System code - international product classification
Embedded EmissionsTotal GHG emissions from production of a good
Default ValuesEU-set emission factors when actual data unavailable
Authorized DeclarantRegistered EU importer permitted to submit CBAM declarations
Carbon LeakageRelocation of production to countries with weaker climate policies
Direct EmissionsGHG released directly from production processes
Indirect EmissionsGHG from electricity consumption in production
